reviews_data.review_text: It may say they close at 10, but they don’t want to help you after 9pm.  Rude, multiple people just didn’t want to help.

If you want to order dinner to go from here you can’t.  If you call they tell you to order online, if you go online they don’t give you an option to order dinner.  It can’t be that hard to answer the phone.

Let me say that I was really looking forward to Reid’s opening in Ballantyne. Unfortunately after purchasing “The Dutch Kettle” Apple butter from them for $11.49 and seeing the same jar at Publix for $5.29 made me question their pricing practices overall. I have no problem with paying higher prices, but this is making me feel something different. I get Publix can likely sell it for less due to scale, but Reid’s pricing it more than double doesn’t make any sense and can’t really be justified. Lost a customer.
The worst. Online ordering never works. Call in orders are ignored or wrong.

Had a lovely time at Reid's this weekend after the mall! The food was delicious, and the atmosphere on the patio was fantastic. A great place to rest after all that shopping! I believe the server's mame was Constance, and she brought her A-game! All the other staff were remarkable as well.

Only disappointment was the lemonade. On previous visits, it was on the sweeter side and was so incredibly good! This time it was more sour/tart and wasn't as appealing. Water is the standard here though, so its not as big a deal though!

This is a really great spot in terms of ambiance and location.  I’ve come here several times with friends and solo.  The food overall is good, however, each time has been met with a dirty piece of silverware, dirty menu, or something floating around in my wine glass.  Those are the little things that can sour a night of dining.  The customer service engagement is phenomenal.  The team is always friendly and accommodating.  One very critical recommendation is for servers to ensure that they know menu items, particularly if dishes contain pork or any other things that could be problematic.  We ordered the brussel sprouts but not aware they had sausage in them.  To conclude on a more positive note, their desserts are phenomenal.  We’ve had the 12 layer chocolate and caramel cake and their blueberry and lemon cheesecake.  Chefs kiss

Sat at the bar recently and had the steak lettuce wraps and brussel sprouts. Both had great flavor with generous portions. The only change they should make...the steak had great flavor, but doesn't work well with Bibb lettuce. Very messy
...kind of like trying to eat a salad with your hands. IMO...should be served with romaine for the wrap. Bartender was awesome.

Came in for an impromptu dinner after seeing it going to Dicks Sporting Goods. Coco was our server, she was soooo personable and sweet. She even spoke with our children and took their orders. On Tuesday’s they have $10 burgers and that’s where they had us, the cocktails and mocktails are also delish!

The store also has a great assortment of condiments to purchase and side dishes. There’s also a small wine cellar in the middle of the store with a professional who discusses the various wines they have in stock. We’ll definitely come back to shop again!
Just a wonderful place period I really enjoyed everything about this place.

This review is specific to the Catering arm of the business. I hosted a dinner party for ten people, and appreciated that they delivered the meal to my house. The apps were delicious (Brie and raspberry phyllo, and crab cakes), and the sides were fine. But the “petite filets with green peppercorn jus” were so overcooked, they were essentially inedible. The meat was so well done and tough, we ended up giving much of it to the dog, and even he had a tough time chewing it. I emailed the person who had helped me place the order, not asking for anything, but simply letting her know that the entree had fallen short of expectations, and I never received any response. After spending over $800 on this occasion, I was extremely disappointed. I will not cater from Reid’s in the future.

I’ll start by saying I’ve been here several times and each time had an increasingly bad experience. The hostesses are rude, bar tenders, and waitresses.

Specifically, the waitress essentially forced my tip at the end saying “it’s $10” while holding the screen. I felt pressured to type that instead of calculate it myself, the. Doing the calculation that I was going to give was much more than I was going to tip. It just felt weird that a waitress was telling me what to pay her, then she blamed her boss because he limits the tip to 15% in the machine unless it’s custom.

The food quality has also gotten worse. Burnt burger bun, and honestly, tasted like a corporate office cook out burger that your boss bought from Walmart.

Sad… because I really do love the space, environment, and goods they sell.

Hire better people please!
Staff, cooks, all of it.
Please

I used to recommend this place from my first couple of experiences years ago, unfortunately I will no longer after this time.

Very unusual for Reid’s. I’ve been coming on Sunday 4-5 years. Food and service always consistent until TODAY 3/8/26. My family and I had the exact meal last weekend (Sunday) and it was delicious. Today, my cosmo was odd color, and second attempt had too much lemon, so I settled for a glass of Malbec. First time experiencing the crab cake (burnt) and so was the hot honey chicken sandwich (overcooked and burnt on the edges. Waitresses are very friendly and eager to please. Again, everyone has a bad day just unexpected. We also love the desserts
left without eating because every plate & glass was dirty. Only reason it got 1 star instead of 0 is because our waitress was very nice
Great staff. Nice breakfast selection, good coffee and comfortable dining area.
Fantastic spot for lunch around SouthPark. Fresh ingredients and the bread is amazing. Love the Poppy Seed Vinaigrette dressing!Highly recommend!
Food was delicious.

Booked on app, unable to accommodate after Hostess suggested we book on app.  Seems to be a disconnect between POS system and no mgmt of front end.

There seems to always be confusion with no mgmt in sight.

Didn’t add extra toppings or sauce on takeout order. Really disappointed that I paid the extra $2 for caramelized onions and did not receive them . Also, there was no visible sauce on the chicken sandwich either. We are frequent Reid’s customers because we do love the quality of the food. However, the last 2 times we’ve been we have had issues with items missing. Beginning to be not worth it.
Outstanding quality and service.

A few of us went to dinner here tonight and can confidently say we will never be returning to this location. We had a new server who was as sweet as can be. She did her best to make our experience enjoyable but the food along with the incredibly rude bartender completely ruined the night. We ordered burgers (two were ordered well done) that came out medium rare. We tried to eat them but ultimately asked if they could be warmed. They brought out two new burgers (we did not ask for new burgers but were unaware ours could not be reheated due to food safety rules), both of which were still far undercooked from what we ordered but we were not going to complain again so we let it go. In the beginning of the night one member of the group ordered a kit royale. The server, being new (again…not her fault) needed clarification on the drink. We informed her it was prosecco and chambord. Our friend was served a glass of prosecco with a shot of chambord on the side. We ordered two more throughout the night which all came the same way. Along comes the bill and it shows the drinks EXTREMELY upcharged. We politely go to the bartender to ask for clarification as we have gone to Reid’s countless times and have never been charged for a $12 shot of chambord on top of the price of the prosecco. She proceeds to push blame to our new server saying she “rang the split up first then asked for the shot”. After going back and forth, the bartender tells us “the kir royale only has a half shot of chambord” to which we responded we are not bartenders so how are we supposed to know this and how is this our fault. She ends up being a smartass saying “well if you come here all the time, you should know or you should’ve come up here and brought it to my attention after the first one”. After multiple issues, not one time did a manager come over to resolve the situation. Staff was getting an attitude and was extremely argumentative. We NEVER asked or even hinted for a meal or drink to be comped other than to make the drink price fair, which didn’t happen. Your bartender needs an attitude adjustment. After countless years coming here, it is beyond disappointing how the situation was handled. You have amazing food but it means nothing if you don’t have decent customer service.

The service, food options, taste of food, the ambience… This can’t be beat. Got a chef’s bowl with shrimp, mixed grilled vegetables, diced sweet potatoes, and crispy brussels. Shout out to Derrick at the chef’s bowl bar. What an outstanding employee. Superior interpersonal skills.!! Thank you!!
Love the chicken salad. Nice staff. Great range of options. Enjoy both locations. Myers doesn't have a restaurant.
Terrible Service.  It appears they have manpower, however without motivation or knowledge.  Unfriendly, it's as if we were doing them a favor to order.  We left and went to Whole foods
Lots of nice speciality items the salmon BLT is the best wrap I've ever had loved it so fresh and full of salmon
Slow and worst service .. took me like 30 minutes to get a breakfast item .. not worth the wait
Reid’s Fine Foods has an amazing breakfast. The quality of the food, the freshness, and the flavors are outstanding. A great spot for a relaxed, delicious morning meal.

Came here one night thinking I would pick up a quick dinner to take back to the hotel and ended up staying for dinner at their restaurant!  The beet, pecan, and caramelized goat cheese was AMAZING. I added a salmon filet and it was cooked to perfection with a crisp outside and still moist on the inside. It was also half priced wbg, so was able to order a nice Pinot Noir to go with my meal.
The next night my colleague joined me and had the salad while I had the burrata appetizer. Crostini was crisp but still soft enough to easily cut pieces to smear the burrata on. Very yummy meal. Definitely saving this place to return to when I return to Charlotte.
We also spent a good deal of time shopping on the grocery side after our meal. Great place!

Love their French bread and their Brown butter chocolate chip cookies. Very expensive tho.
An example is they have Racine Danish Kringle. A staple for this displaced Midwestern girl. Reid's price is $24.95!! I can get the same kringle from Traders Joe's $10 cheaper.

Full service local food market , with butcher shop and deli plus so much more!
Big selection of cakes and desert's...
Great produce!
1st visit. Place reminds me of the shops at Colonial Williamsburg, Virginia.  Highly recommended.  You will not regret the visit. This may become one of your favorite places to visit.
What an awesome spot.  Highly recommend
I really love this place. I ordered my Thanksgiving dinner from  here. So impressed by the quality of food.

I usually go to the Selwyn location because it’s closer but was by the mall today so ordered ahead at SouthPark. The wrap is so disappointingly filled…. I’ve taken three bites and already hit just a huge amount of plain wrap. I’ll not be returning to this location as Selwyn has never filled it so poorly for $12…. Extremely disappointed. I love these wraps and was really looking forward to it. :(

I purchased the brussel sprouts gratin and it was inedible. The gratin was floating in grease. I took 2 bites and threw it all away. I love brussels but this was just awful. A waste of $20. I also purchased 2 pies from them and I hope they are better. Very disappointing quality.
Louise is excellent waitress
Excellent..excellent.. the chicken caprese was beyond what I expected. Money well spent and look forward to coming back.
Fine meats and cheeses with great service
Love to drop in here for dessert
Beautiful 😍 store with lots of good options.

Well, my first experience with Reid’s was kind of annoying. Order a pressed sandwich that took over 30 minutes to get. No apology was given. Don’t go there at lunchtime unless you pre-order would be my advice. Also not the cheapest place in the world so I would’ve expected a little bit better customer service.🤷🏻‍♀️

Amazing deal on burgers on Tuesdays. 10.99 for a steak burger and fries.
Other lunch special during the week are great also

Waited 15 minutes for 2 sandwiches, when it wasn't crowded. Two sandwiches, two chips, and a large container of crackers was $65. Waited with the 20 minutes I had at the bar to have a beer. Was passed by 4 times without a word. I was the only one at the bar, and only 3 tables were occupied. Not the first time like that at the bar. The sandwich folks and folks at the register were nice. 1/10 would recommend.
Best Place In Southpark

Great food, cozy atmosphere, a true Charlotte institution.  We used to partake in their Tuesday burger specials when we lived here. Now we are here for breakfast.  A wonderful omelette with feta cheese.

Excellent high-quality ingredient grocery store with a restaurant.  You know you're getting the best when you shop here, and it comes with a very welcoming laid back environment.  The restaraunt's food was also great.  Super nice staff.
Young gentleman behind the bakery counter was very pleasant on my first visit and today when I picked up my order. It’s so nice when someone smiles and is pleasant.

The food that they serve in the restaurant is just the food that they sell in the store, so you would get a better value from just shopping around instead of sitting down and dining. Their chocolate vanilla cake is really good. The Charcuterie board was just okay. It was very hard and I would have liked to see better cheeses and more variety.
Great service and food.

Placed an order for pick up for the monte cristo special and they did not put dijonaise or bacon in my sandwich, they also didn’t include the house chips. The bread was also SOGGY! will not be ordering from here again. 1/10 do not recommend.
Amazing food and staff!
Delicious lunch special burgers! Great atmosphere!

I am sick of this place and I’m done here. Several times I have gotten a salad with little to no dressing and this last time both salads had no dressing at all and they were both on the skimpy side. I get it mistakes happen but $35 plus dollars for two salads and this keeps happening, nope. See ya Reids, it been a mediocre run!
This is such a gorgeous gem and South Park staple. Sit at the bar and get a Aperol Spritz and double decker strawberry shortcake (which is actually cheesecake FYI) from Sandy - she is wonderful!
We love Reid's!! 💚
Stupid expensive but high quality

I have to say, i am pretty disappointed in Reids. This is the third time i have gone in to get various items, Franks pimento cheese, pork bbq, chicken bbq, smoked gouda pimento cheese, pre-made casseroles, etc and they are out. Practically nothing on the shelves other than items you can buy at any grocery store. I spend at $175-250 in the store every week or other week on steaks, chicken, etc. I’m very happy they are doing great business, but come on, have some stuff on the shelves to purchase that i come there for other than meat. If this continues i guess ill have to start shopping at the Butchers Market.

We have always loved Reid’s!! Each time we are in Charlotte (frequently) we make sure to include a stop. Tonight was THE most disappointing experience we have ever had. Food was not prepared as ordered, but most of all, the biggest disappointment was the waitress. Hostess was lovely, other waitstaff smiling. Ours was surly, delivered less than desiresble service on every level.
We drive over an hour to buy the brisket burgers! Photo is the burger made at home, I haven't eaten in the restaurant.

First time dining at Reid's.
The service was friendly.
Our food was fabulous.
Just loved the patio area!

We have visited Reid's several times in the last month with family. Most everyone ordered something different and everyone was very pleased with their meals.  It was fresh and delicious. Both times we have received wonderful service.  The burgers and pasta are two of my favorites. Try it, you won't be disappointed.
Always consistent in the quality of food. Also Salted Melon is phenomenal.
The butcher dept staff and the deli counter are wonderfully professional

Reid's is consistently the best place I ever eat. Today, it was our choice of restaurant for Father's day and it did not disappoint. Everything was delicious and fresh. I will continue to come back time and time again!
Been going to Reids all-my-life

We had brunch at south park location and got molded food. The waiter acknowledged it and we were still charged. We emailed the restaurant twice but so far no any reply. When you are eating, be careful their food.
Great selection of fine foods, wines, cheeses, pastas and a great restaurant. Excellent burgers
Went for dinner for a friend's birthday. Excellent service, food was great and the patio was fun.

food is good and so is the variety of items you can pick from, however the quality of service makes the food forgettable. at the counter where you order sandwiches and salads, the workers are very rude. i asked to make my own sandwich and get a cup of soup and the lady help me had the worst attitude and for no reason. and the setup of the restaurant/shop is very confusing. it’s weird how you can order salads, sandwiches, and soup but you can only sit outside. and then to order off of the menu you have to be sat in the dining room with the hostess, like why not just combine everything? it’s unnecessarily complicated. when trying to figure out the process i asked the hostess, and she didn’t know what she was talking about so that was a bust. i ended up sitting down with a friend to order food, and after 15 minutes of waiting on someone to help us we had to get up and say we want to order food, meanwhile there was only two other occupied tables in the whole restaurant. again, food is very good so in the future i’d probably just order takeout.

We put in reservations a long time ago. We Requested inside seats. We were told “should not be a problem you are the 2 group to sign up for Mothers Day.” We get there for Mothers Day and we are told we HAVE to sit out side. She takes us to the table and chairs and table are still wet from night of raining. They want me to sit in a wet chair an eat off wet table. NO WAY!! So we GET  to sit at the bar in hard uncomfortable stools. Food was ok nothing to write home about. Then at bar I note that no one is washing their hands. Then using ice scoop and dipping it back down in ice. Cross contamination big time. To put the screws to it the people they sat as we were leaving she said” the people who scheduled with us first get to pick inside or out first.
A Charlotte Institution, a great restaurant and a fun (if pricey) packaged and prepared goods market.

Fantastic dinner experience! On Tuesday’s there’s a burger deal, and also you have the option to pick out a bottle of wine from the shop if it’s not on the standard menu, to have with your meal. Of course you must get a slice of cheesecake picked out from the display of desserts to top it off. Everything was delicious and the patio is very quaint

The deli counter could really benefit from a more organized system. It’s often unclear where to stand, who’s already ordered, and who’s next—leading to people accidentally cutting in line or grabbing the wrong sandwiches. A simple number system or clearer ordering / pickup process would make a big difference. The food is great, but the chaos takes away from the experience.
I dress down. Got it. I may look like I don't belong. I'm willing to bet my bank account does. It feels like I'm being watched every time I visit, which is to get foie Gras that's never in stock.

I recently had the pleasure of dining at Reid’s Restaurant Wine & Bar, and I can confidently say it was an experience worth sharing. From the moment we walked in, the atmosphere was warm and inviting, setting the perfect tone for a fantastic meal.

A special shoutout to Carly, our server, whose exceptional service truly elevated our afternoon. She was attentive, knowledgeable, and had a genuine enthusiasm that made us feel welcomed and well taken care of. Her recommendations were spot on, and her attention to detail did not go unnoticed.

The food was nothing short of outstanding—beautifully presented and bursting with flavor. Whether you’re stopping by for a casual lunch or a special occasion, Reid’s delivers on all fronts.

If you’re looking for a place with top-notch service, a great atmosphere, and an unforgettable dining experience, Reid’s Restaurant Wine & Bar is the spot. Ask for Carly—you won’t be disappointed!
Party of two enjoyed a nice breakfast.

Fun store for gourmet food.

Great deli and wine selection.

Staff is very attentive and kind, but perishable food (e.g. cheese, spinach, other produce, bottled fresh juices, etc.) is often outdated. When we've shared with staff, for example, that today is January 23 but product says "use by January 16," the product is removed that particular time, but similarly outdated items remain next time. We've seen this happen over a couple of years, with a staff member even telling us to be careful of expired food. Tasty food overall, but if Reid isn't more careful, customers could get very sick. More quality control is needed.

Fried oysters were ok. Not hot and fresh. Live music was a bit loud. We usually have a better experience at Reid’s. Excellent selection of meats, cheeses, wines and desserts. We went there for dinner and usually go for lunch. Outside seating is nice, weather permitting.
Good quality food and a great spot for casual dining and for lunch! Tuesday has a half-price buger special. It's one of my favorite places to go with my yoga friends
The veggie risotto with seared scallops was divine

I was excited for this because I’d heard great things about Reid’s. My Caesar salad wrap was soggy and had no taste. Way too much wrap to salad/chicken ratio - so underwhelming. I definitely will never go back.
Very cool vibe and good food

I’ve been a regular shopper at REIDS for quite some time now, whether it’s for a quick lunch or to pick up groceries. Unfortunately, over my recent visits, I’ve become increasingly disappointed by the decline in both the quality of products and the overall standards of the store.

One consistent issue is the deli department. Each time I visit, the staff seems either rude or overwhelmed, likely due to being understaffed, with only one employee trying to handle everything. This isn’t just about poor service – it’s about an establishment that seems to struggle with meeting basic customer needs.

A more concerning issue is the state of the prepared foods. On multiple occasions, I’ve noticed flies and palmetto bugs in the refrigerated section where the prepared foods are stored. This raises serious questions about hygiene and food safety. Additionally, the quality of the food is never consistent, often lacking in freshness and taste.

To make matters worse, I’ve found that many of the products in the prepared foods section are frequently past their expiration dates. Sometimes they are out of date by just a day, but other times, it’s been as much as a week. This shows a clear disregard for quality control and customer health.

It’s incredibly disappointing that a store I’ve trusted for so long has let its standards fall this far. I’m genuinely shocked that REIDS continues to operate successfully while letting such important issues slide.

I regret to say this will be my last visit until I see significant changes in both the quality of your food and the standards of your customer service. I’ve attached photos and videos to support my claims, and I hope these concerns are addressed seriously. Good luck.

Restaurant / Bar / Wine store / Meat store / Cheese shop / Pastries !!!   All good!
Don't Go Mondays! Its not a restaurant on mkndays! You buy your stuff, and you can sit down to eat. No service. The bar is closed too.
Server named Jennifer was so kind and efficient. Soup and burger special were so delicious.
Have always been a been a huge fan of Reid’s, but the last few times I’ve been were terrible. Food and customer service have largely declined, sadly.
Meat selection in the grocery is great quality. The restaurant is lovely especially outside on the patio when there is live music.
Love this place. Friendly staff and amazing patio! Go for the super chill live music! Food is delicious and vibe is on point.
I’m rating Sandy the bartender she was a one woman show waiting in a full bar with a smile and friendly attentive to everyone while being her own bar back as well I’d definitely go back

I've been to this establishment several times, both to eat & to shop. However, l've noticed that within the last two weeks while eating that a manager, Kristen, (short caucasian woman/blonde, large bun, early 40s) was yelling at servers while on the floor. I've been waited on with no issues with wait staff, however, I find that the management yelling at waitresses/waiters in front of customers is not only unprofessional but shows how this establishment treats their staff and runs their businesses. My family & I will no longer be returning due to how staff is treated/spoken to by management.|
Sandy at Reid’s Fine Foods was amazing.  They always have the best food and ambiance but Sandy takes it to another level!!!

Pretty disappointed…. Both my water glass and wine glass were dirty, both had dried chunks of stuff stuck to the glass and one even had lip prints on it. I also ordered the Chicken Caesar Wrap and it didn’t come as requested but I ate it since they all seemed pretty busy. The only reason I’m giving two stars is because the Caesar dressing and the chicken itself were very good. Unfortunately, I don’t see myself returning to Reid’s in the future.
Great lunch options. Covered patio made it Great to sit outside andThe covered
Very Nice Restaurant!
Had the best experience! Came in with a couple of friends on Sunday and Kristin the bartender was fabulous. She took such good care of us, we will be back again!

Kristen B was wonderful. She recommended the Chai espresso martini and it was fantastic. She was very personable but also very professional and quick. Absolutely recommended going for the brunch cocktails and to visit Kristen B. Will be back !
Lauren made me the yummiest salad on Tuesday!! Quality customer service!

You can't beat the combo of quality and service! Henry went above and beyond to show us some incredible cheese options and was super accomodating and kind to my pregnant wife. Never a bad experience here!
Really good food!! You should definitely try the Mediterranean chicken wrap!!
This place deserves no stars everything is overpriced, small, you can't even find the place to checkout
Food was great.  Very much appreciated the chef who came to our table to discuss preparation of a meal for a child with food allergies.
Had dinner there last week with family.   The food was amazing and Peyton the wine manager suggested the absolute best wines.   Would highly recommend and we'll definitely be back.
Wonderful experience! From the restaurant staff to Peyton in the wine shop providing excellent recommendations, we had a great time and enjoyed ourselves!

Lots of great wine, snack, deli and prepared food options. We browsed the place (so fun) and then bought a bottle of wine. We grabbed a couple glasses from the bartender and drank the wine while we sat outside in the sun. Fun!

Great spot for brunch! The menu has a wide selection that will please most. The staff is friendly, and the food tasted good! I love going for wine Wednesday’s as well as burger Tuesdays. Make sure you get there early if you pick either of those days.

My Fiancé and I decided to have lunch, earlier in the week and, we  had the pleasure of receiving outstanding service from Michael, and I couldn't be more impressed. His professionalism, knowledge, and attention to detail made my experience truly exceptional. He went above and beyond to ensure that all my questions were answered and that I was completely satisfied with the service that he was able to provide as a representation of the Reid's brand. We are looking forward to our next visit when we are back in Charlotte!

cheers!
Cheryl was so attentive tonight and the food was great. Live music on a Thursday is a great reason to come back!

First, we have loved Reeds since we moved to Charlotte 5 years ago. Great food, ambiance, outdoor dining and winelist. We had dinner with friends, we have not see for awhile last evening, and unfortunatly, due to the music from the duo that was playing in the dining room, it was impossible to have a conversation. I will be back, but not on nights they have music. Our freinds were disappointed.

Our second trip to Reid's for a meal and once again are disappointed. Today we went for the New Year's Eve brunch. My husband got the Banana Foster's pancakes and I got the Oysters Rockefeller.  I have many an Oyster Rockefeller and was so looking forward to these. They were recommended by the wait staff but to say I was disappointed was to put it mildly.  Upon arrival, the oysters looked great covered with sauce and parmesan cheese.  The spinach was slim to none but the worst of all was that the oysters were barely cooked. Then my husband's banana's foster was a bust.  Reid's would have done better by offering pancakes with a half banana on the side.  It was not cooked and definitely didn't deserve a banana foster heading. I have given Reid's a second try and don't think they deserve a third.
No reason to spend the kind of money it costs to shop here unless you need to prove to yourself or someone else you're reckless with your money.
Gourmet foods at gourmet prices.

This isn’t a review to humiliate anyone or this restaurant overall. The pieces of steak on the steak bowl are too big and too full of gristle to eat. I had to spit out much of it. Couldn’t chew it up. Dangerous too.

Don't order anything to be delivered from this restaurant.  It will not come as you ordered it, IF it comes at all.  They never get it right and I have been trying to order delivery at lunch time from this company for over 3 years. Today is the last time.  It is always wrong and if you call and complain, the manager will tell you that she or he will send you a gift card for the inconvenience, and that never happens either.

Not a bad spot if you go in person but it is overpriced in store as well.  Just never order delivery you will be so disappointed.
Nicole was our server and she was great!!  So kind and doting on our table!

This place is real cool. You can order fresh from the store of jummy goodies or dine in in the restaurant. Our waitress,  Cheryl, was pheninomal. She educated us on this new hot whisky that's out. We enjoyed it all.
The chicken salad tastes good but the sandwich is half lettuce. Spent 13 on a sandwich with barely any Chicken salad on it, No chips or anything. This is SO disappointing and over priced.
Reid’s at South Park is the best! Taylor Campbell manages the store well and does an awesome job!
Our waiter Eliza was very helpful and courteous. He was there when we needed him without being overbearing. My wife and I were very happy with the goat cheese salad and the steak burger.
Always a wonderful time at Reid’s SP. Sat outside at the outdoor bar today and had a great time chatting with our server,, CHERYL G! Great service, great food… ALWAYS!!!
Reid’s at South Park is well-managed by Taylor Campbell! Great displays and delicious food.
Reid’s at Southpark is great! Taylor Campbell does an awesome job!
Louise our waitress was awesome and the food was fresh and the wine was great too!

Love to shop at Reids at Southpark! I was informed by General Manager Taylor Campbell who is so informed and very helpful that Reids supports local small businesses and actually how much of the products are produced in house. Everything is always so fresh and the meat department offers excellent cuts and the seafood is outstanding. Highly recommend the restaurant as well and they have great selection of wines by the glass or bottle. Great atmosphere!
⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️⭐️
Great food and atmosphere!

This places leaves a lot to be desired. Kitchen help, and I say help, because in no way are they chefs, can't read and get an
order correctly.
Great place. Great service. Sandy makes awesome drinks!

From the deli I ordered the salmon BLT, and my husband ordered the blackened buffalo chicken wrap.

The salmon was dry and bland with zero seasoning (not even salt). The lettuce, tomato, and bread were SOGGY, and there was waaayyyy too much mayo. I would never order this again.

On my husband’s wrap, the chicken was also bland and unseasoned, and was not blackened at all.

At the deli I saw at least 5 live flies flying around where there was open food, such as a pan of bread, another of roasted potatoes, and yet another of Brussels sprouts.

Where the canned sodas and kombucha are displayed, there were 2-3 DEAD flies on the shelf with the drinks.

Over $50 later, we were extremely displeased and turned off by the food and overall experience of the place. The dessert was good but only safe as long as flies aren’t in the dessert display with the food.

We’ve eaten here before, and after today decided not to eat here again. :/

Reids is always my go to place to wind down, watch a game, celebrate special family occasions and more!  Behind every great place is a great boss!  Dee, you are the best and at the realm is a boss that makes that happen!

Great experience being there for first time with my co-worker. Staff is very friendly and proffesional. We had all our questions answered and were helped by great suggestions. All staff we met were so kind.... special shout to out to this lady (Sydney Smith) whom we met and made our experience unique. Love this place and will def come back and recommend for anyone looking a great dining place....

I stopped in here for the first time the other day and was taken care of by the friendliest and most professional young woman I’ve met in a long time; Sydney S. She was very busy but had no problem stopping to help me with all my questions and even made a few suggestions for me which I appreciated. The world needs more people like this around!  Thank you Sydney for making my visit so incredible!  When I’m back in the area I will be sure to stop by again!

I recently placed a catering order for my company with Reid’s and had an incredible experience. The entire staff worked with me to ensure all of my needs were met. Special shout out to their assistant GM Sydney for working with me to make special accommodations for allergies and food preferences. She was there every step of the way. Thank you Reid’s and Sydney!
Extremely rude staff, specifically at the sandwich shop. Yelled at us because were in the wrong line.

Expensive but lovely grocery with hot food and an outside patio.  Clean food available for those on a cut.

Self serve counter payment with tap to pay available.

Deli bar is very good - Known as a grocery store, the deli bar delivers some excellent salads and sandwiches.  The Cuban revivals those from Florida.  Salads have a huge range of optional ingredients.  Good place to get fresh sandwiches and salads

Cool vibe and convenient after shopping at the Soutpark Mall! It's a combination of a market, bar and sandwhich shoppe. They offer some wine tastings 2x a month so I would recommend checking it out. Pretty family friendly but also a good spot for lunch or working lunch vibe.

I had meatloaf,  potatoes, and brussels sprouts. Everything was very delicious and flavorful . The service was very kind and friendly.
I will definitely return to try more items!
Today was our first visit, and we will certainly be back. The food was fantastic and the service was amazing. Matthew K. took excellent care of our table. The salmon salad was divine. Thank you!

I wanted to try something different,  I saw this place. Horrible.  I ordered a Cesar's salad with shrimp. Online, it doesn't say grill or uncooked on any of the proteins. When I received it, it was a shrimp cocktail.  My lettuce was very brown with a bad taste to it. I had more cheese than anything. I had little to no dressing in the side cup. I'm very disappointed in this order. Smh. My coworkers ordered salads as well. They weren't, please at all. For $80, it wasn't worth it at all. 😒

Very good food, especially love their steak burgers. Louise is a terrific waitress who I always ask for, and Rhonda is a great bartender and makes a wicked good martini. I find myself going there every week or two. They have lovely outdoor patio seating.

We moved back from Atlanta and have been searching for a great place to eat and drink that is also dog friendly. Half of wine on Saturdays, covered patio, healthy food options and GREAT WINE! Service is incredible and they also have a great grocery section w/ fresh cuts of meat and cheese.

Our favorite Saturday spot, great food, service, patio, and half of bottles of wine!!! It truly *does not get better* than Reid’s. The staff is so friendly and we love the covered patio with our pup. Also, find myself here on Thursday’s for live music as well. 10/10 .. will be back next Saturday and the Saturday after that and the Saturday after that.. so on so forth..

A really good place for a sunny day after shopping at southpark. The service is really good and everyone is courteous, welcoming.

They got good wine list perfect for sunny evening.
Tried their quinoa kale salad which was really good. It be even better if had some more dressing.
Nashville hot chicken is not the best in town but satisfactory. Truffle fries are must and really good.
Bean burger patty was really good and 4/5

Will recommend this for a summer afternoon or evening for sure.
Friendly staff, quick service, local beers (Legion) on tap, decent sandwiches. Worthy a visit.
I’ve been here to dine a couple of times now and I swear each time I’ve come in the staff are bickering or making snide comments towards each other, I’m surprised they’re not short staff.

Came here for the first time just the other day. It was a little bit hard to find coming from inside the mall. Glad we found it. We both got sandwiches from the deli and they were absolutely fabulous. I had the spicy Italian on a baguette and it was truly mouthwatering. We sat outside on the patio and it was lovely. I pursued the selection of wine and cheeses while waiting for our sandwiches. I must say they had quite the collection. Would love to come back another time to take a more in depth look.
The closest thing to France that I’ll be for awhile. Fun and cute!

We ate here for dinner following the Charlotte half marathon. Had an absolute blast and fantastic service. Appetizers, entrees, and cocktails were top tier. Our server Laurie and the music was a delight. Will come back.

The glasses for drinks are always weirdly cloudy and look dirty. The bathrooms are also not that clean for a place that charges $70 for a Reid's grocery tote bag. The staff at the bar seem to be preoccupied with tasks and not very professional or knowledgeable. But the deli area is fantastic, as is the staff working it, and they do have a brilliant selection of other items, such as dry pastas, meats, and various goods. This place is great but could be better if some minor adjustments to other details were addressed.

Nice atmosphere, food was good, duo music wonderful. Will return for better seating earlier. Waitstaff brought out entrées before the appetizer. Took this home with us. Overall a good experience, just need better training of waitstaff.
Great lunch spot with outstanding sandwiches. Good atmosphere and friendly staff!
I have been going to Reid's at Selwyn for many, many years.   Prepared foods are excellent and staff unbelievably professional and service oriented - Harrison Singer especially.    Real Solid.
Always enjoy going into Reid’s.   Food is great and customer service is exceptional.    Selwyn location is the best.   Harrison is a great employee

For our party of eight, Rhonda was our best friend. The food was excellent, but most on our minds as we left was the professional care she offered that caused us to want to return. This was the PERFECT case of 5 STAR customer service. Gary
Great market! Great sandwiches!  Customer service was fantastic!

This staff needs some retraining. I was with my elderly mother taking her to lunch earlier this afternoon. My mother has mobility issues and trouble standing for long periods. After nearly being run over by several servers, the hostess finally appears and states. “We have a wait.” That’s fine, and to be expected. I state we’ll just need a seat for my mom. The hostess rears up and as pretentious as possible restates “We have a wait.“ The appropriate response would have been, No problem we can get you a chair while you wait. Not the obnoxious response we received. we left and will not retur. I feel you owe an apology to my mother for you callous behavior.
Duck legs, duck fat, Italian lambrusco and triple creme brie = Christmas dinner sorted! Excellent service and amazing selection of gourmet foods! Thanks to the staff at the meat counter!
Worst Thanksgiving dinner ever!  Except the tenderloin.

I absolutely love Reids. Before or after visiting the Shopping mall, I always stop in to explore what Reids has or to pick up dessert for my family and I. Reids comes to mind first if ever I desire to gift someone wine upon arrival to their home, prepare wine and chacuterie board or celebrate a special occasion. The wine selection is exceptional.
The caviar, fish, meat, and cheese selections are also consistent with this standard.
This Gormet grocery store is truly a gem in Charlotte and is much appreciated for it's fine quality.

For 𝐃𝐢𝐯𝐢𝐧𝐞-𝐃𝐢𝐧𝐢𝐧𝐠 & 𝐋𝐮𝐱𝐮𝐫𝐲📸 follow
IG: Torah.Divine
One of our go-to’s in Southpark, always have a great time on their beautiful patio. Live music on the patio Wed-Sunday. Food and service always top notch.

My salad was phenomenal. However, the box that held my salad was disgusting. If I weren't so hungry and didn't have to go back to work, I would have asked them to do everything over. It looked as if someone wiped their filthy hands all over the box. I prayed extra hard over my food and use hand sanitizer to wipe the box off. Presentation is everything!!
Delish., any time a black bean burger is good, with a side of mustard coleslaw. 5 🌟's everytime.
Really nice, well-curated deli/bakery. They have a great selection of unique and local items.
The service at the hot plate bar is amazing. Ms. K and her colleagues continuously provide great meals in a very sincere and genuine manner. 10/10 recommend.

The customer service is horrible especially the cashiers in the morning! They act like they do not want to help you or maybe it's not help anyone but people of color, this will be my last time ever going there!

The food is good, love the look of the store and concept, but the Manager ( I am assuming) didn’t have a chef coat, very rude, I was in line and the server the person behind me, the person behind have enough sense to asked me I have been served.
The manager proceeded to asked another associates to help the lady behind me, she has been in line( not the case at all)
The appropriate thing to do is to ask how’s next.
And not assume. ( looks or color if skin can’t fool you)  I will go again and order the same sandwich ( tuna melt)
Love coming here for dessert, cheese, and easy-to-make dinner.
Nice little market for charcuterie
Great food and nice atmosphere

I entered Reid's at 6:40pm on a Sunday. Instead of walking up to me, some half-crazed tatted employee with a mustache screamed, " We're closed!" Google shows they close at 8pm. Worst ever-- I was being told to leave for no reason. If the deli is closed, it should be posted on their website, in their store and Google. Their hours on Google are falsely advertised as of 5/1/2022.
Bad customer service

I love the salads and sandwiches at Reid's South Park.  Ms. K made my salad the last two times I visited, and she is so nice.  The salads are sold by the pound and they're made to order.  I can literally tell Ms. K how many grape tomatoes I would like in my salad (3 or 4), and she opens her hand to show me that's how many she put in there.  The sandwiches are delicious too.  They have hot or cold sandwiches. My husband likes the Cuban sandwich and it is served hot.  There is outdoor seating, so we've been able to enjoy that as well.  I highly recommend Reid's Fine Foods.

TL: DR
Overpriced food. Bad service.

The grocery store is pretty nice, but remember that you're paying a premium because you're at South Park. Really do enjoy going to this grocery store, but there are so many other places that do it better.

The problem is is that the food warrants the location but not the actual price. It's cooked properly but the ingredients aren't exactly any better than anywhere else will find. A few times I've been here, The meat is cooked properly, but everything else was no better or less than you can expect at any chance. I don't think I've ever had a properly cooked french fry, they're almost always chewy and old.

But the main problem is that they do not have a kitchen that's nearly robust enough for the amount of tables that they try to serve. If they're at half capacity or higher you're going to be waiting ages for service, and longer for orders. Which, under normal circumstances would probably be fine, but they do not communicate this with you and your server will never be seen other than to come grab your order and bring stuff out. I have waited over half an hour after finishing a beer, wondering if my server will come back to refill water or get another drink for me.

I don't have a problem with slow service as long as it's communicated before I sit down so I have the option of saying maybe not today. But I won't be back. I'm tired of dealing with this places inability to communicate.

We were able to get reservations for six here for an early evening meal on Saturday, 4/9/2022. The place does seem like a corner deli with tables on the side for eating. The tables in the seating area were crowded but we were glad to find a place to enjoy a meal. The waiter was attentive and friendly. There was a limited mixed drink menu. I tried one of the Moscow Mules which I enjoyed. We ordered a couple of appetizers, the pretzel bites with beer cheese and the deviled eggs. They weren't extra large eggs but were flavorful. The beer cheese seemed more like a gravy than a cheese dip to me but was ok. I ordered the steak frites. It was a tender filet but had no seasoning. My wife had the oysters and grilled asparagus. The oysters were small but had a nice dipping sauce. She said that the asparagus was not the best and left half of it uneaten. My daughter let me have one of her crab cakes. I couldn't taste much crab in it and didn't finish it. A young lady started playing a guitar and singing after we got there which we enjoyed. We bought some small desserts from the deli on the way out. We were happy to get to enjoy a meal with family and enjoy each other's company.

I stood in line so I can get a salad. There was no one in front of me there was a lady next to me and getting prepared foods. Workers were milling about. No one acknowledged me. I waited over four minutes without being acknowledged or even asked what I wanted. And now when I went to the checkout to tell them that I was going to leave because no one was waiting on me the two people at the check out were on their cell phones.
I left to go get my dinner someplace else that wanted my business versus a place that doesn’t care about business. I come here regularly fir work and I do like getting a salad from here for my dinner one of the nights I’m working. Guess I’ll find someplace else

The deli took over 30 minutes to make 3 sandwiches. Unkind waitress Emily would not allow deli customers to sit at the tables outside, saying it was only for reservations. This has never happened before here.

We had to eat the sandwiches at the mall food court. You can get better customer service at Walmart.
Small grocery store. Nice selection but a bit pricey.
Great concept, love the food and location. Will be back

Usually the food is very good. Last Saturday  ,my wife's  crab cakes were cool by the time they got to the table and the remoulade sauce overpowered the other items on the plate.  The server took the plate back to be reheated and the manager checked by the table twice to be sure everything was satisfactory.

Love Reid’s! I just wish they’d offer more organic options (reflected in the 4 stars vs. 5). I’m the daughter and granddaughter of grocers from Detroit. Grew up in the business. I really like the old fashioned stores. We weren’t too hungry so we had our usual roast beef sandwich but we asked them to do it like a panini. It was delicious! I think we’ll have a drink the next time we visit South Park Mall. Stop by Reid’s, take it slow. Enjoy!
Not going back. Tonight was the second poor experience   Staff just stands around behind the salad / sandwich counter. Selwyn location is much better- by far.

Charlotte NC legacy!  Outstanding quality butcher, food store.  Great cuts of meat and large selection of accoutrements.  Dine in restaurant and bar have high quality food, prepared well.  Price is equal to quality of food and service.  Burger special ( Tuesday nights ) is a must to. Bar area is welcoming and ample Atiba for the sports fans.
Great food store.
First time attendee,went on Sunday for brunch, did not enjoy the food. Sorry
Great desserts and beer. Nice atmosphere. Bartender was good. Met a nice couple at the bar. Nice live music

Our server was very helpful and nice, but could not make up for the rest of the experience. One of our appetizer plates was very dirty, with about a 2 inch piece of wet food on it . The calamari fries were overcooked, and 1 of the 3 burgers were overcooked.
Great butcher.

Great service,  and good food.
Will try the restaurant again.
Definitely want to visit the store, and really look around.

Great specialty shop! Also plenty of offerings for lunch and other meals of the day. One thing and it's my fault for not looking or asking, however the chicken salad is more like chicken salad mousse...... :( next time its cold cuts!
Our server Chaz was awesome.   I will come back.

This is a nice place to have brunch.  They have an outside seating area which is a plus for me during COVID-19.   They also sell meats and fish that can be purchased and taken home to cook. The prices are very reasonable for brunch.

ordered takeout, we were given no forks. along with no sausage for the breakfast bowls and no syrup or bacon for the french toast. needed salt for the breakfast bowl. French toast was way overly sweet
Enjoyed the Cheese & Charcuterie board and we had 4 different dishes they were all good.

Paid $10 for a sandwich and look at the quality of the produce. The cheese was ripped in half and dried out like it hadn’t been sealed properly. The tomato was mush and I wouldn’t even feed a bunny that lettuce. The bacon was pre cooked microwave cardboard. So very disappointed.

The restaurant has a quaint atmosphere with a large bar and a nice outdoor patio. The steak burger is incredible. I recommend getting it with pimento cheese. They also have a great selection of specialty foods and meats.

I can't believe I had never been here until recently!  Stopped in this week to pick up a $15 bottle of wine for a friend and a wrap in the deli case, and both were so delicious!  Love that is a local Charlotte place with lots of regional products.

My husband and I are choosy about wine and food, and everything we've had has been insanely good and well priced. We used to live closer to Laurel Market and loved their sandwiches (and bacon!) and Reid's gives them a run for their money (the turkey/bacon/avocado sandwich on a croissant is incredible) . We will be frequent customers!
Only bought a boxed salad, but the friendly people there helped me get the things needed to safely microwave leftovers at our hotel.

The array of specialty foods in the market was amazing.  I wish I was staying in town longer so I could buy everything.

The restaurant was closed when I went so or ordered a sandwich frim the deli.  Without hesitation the man at the counter suggested turkey avocado bacon sandwich.  It was heavenly. For $9 it can easily feed two people.   It was so good I ate the whole thing and wanted more.  One more day in Charlotte and will try to go from breakfast.   That good!!!
Wonderful selection, top quality, helpful and accommodating staff
Always the best salads and wraps!
Went there at 5 pm today. Great service from our waiter. The steak burger and crab cakes was amazing. So was their old fashioned mule cocktail.
Reid's is great.  Nice market to get a few niche things one might need. Ate lunch inside but service was sub par.  Disappointing but all the food was good.

Vegan options! I ordered the bean burger no mayo and it came with Mayo on it and no avocado. Wasn’t the best bean burger I’ve ever had but it was okay. Wine selection was good. Vegan dessert wasn’t half bad.
Wonderful customer service from the manager, Dee. The chicken pomodoro is simply ah-amazing! Goodness, thank you for such good tasting food!

Reid's Fine Foods was a great place to have lunch. You order your food at the deli and pay and sit inside or outside. For dinner you have a table and server and the dinner menu is delicious!! Very clean and safe!
Great place to grab a quick delicious sandwich 🥪😋
Always good food

Very good burger to go was missing toppings.  Must be common since they had me check the order before I left.  They were very busy and it took a while to get my food, but it was good.   There was a good char on the outside of the burger and cooked to order.
Great place for brunch.
Wonderful food and service!
Always a good sandwich and friendly staff

Service is low, food is good but not great.
Waiters are nice, bartender Belinda is kinda rude and rolls her eyes whenever I asked for something, whether it was a refill or some ranch dip. She Asked me multiple times to move to free a table, even though I was by myself it was rude the way she came across.

No matter how much variety a store has, if the customer service is poor - it’s pulls down everything else. We wanted to buy beef short ribs and the butcher picked out ones that were old and dry and when we asked for different cuts - he gave a rude response that it was all taken out today and slammed them rudely in the bag. When we cooked it - it was clearly spoiled meat. Whole Foods is a quarter mile over and what a difference in customer service.  So we won’t be buying anything from this store ever and they just lost a customer
Awesome variety of high end products. Knowledgeable staff.
Note that the store closes earlier than the restaurant. And their closing time on Google is for the restaurant
Great place!
Great burger deals

Struggled with a 3 or four star on this one. Decided to go 4 stars based on taste of food alone. This was a stop on our Charlotte Burger tour and a stop I've been wanting to make for a while as I've never been to this long standing Charlotte establishment.
On entering I was in love. I will always love a small grocery store and think it would be a great place for a fun date. Pick out your meal, take it home and cook! Lots of fun options you won't find everywhere and what appears to be fresh ones too.
We were sat quickly inside at a table with two screens which was very hard to get into. I barely fit between the screens and the table and I'm not a large person. I am not sure how others are making it happen. This is due to covid restrictions, I'm sure, so no points taken away for this.
We sat for a while. At least 5 minutes, but likely longer before being approached by a waiter. We ordered wine and sat even longer before it arrived. In the meantime we realized quickly the acoustics are terrible and it was very hard to hear each other talk, however, we could hear clearly the people across from us.
Once we got our wine, we ordered all of our food to get things rolling. A while later (we started to realize things must be slow here) our calamari fries and brussel sprouts came out...to the table next to us.  After they looked at it and breathed all over it, (I hate when this happens, especially during these times) we realized it was ours and informed the server who quicky gave it to us.
I loved every bite of both of these appetizers.  The brussels were some of the best I have had. They were perfectly cooked and had a wonderful glaze. The calamari was definitely different. Nice and tender with great breading and a lovely thai sauce. The portions were big and we were quickly full.
Being full soon became nothing to worry about as it took 30 more minutes to get our Burger. It first came out....again to the people next to us.  We realized it instantly but couldn't get the servers attention before the other table grabbed it. Now, in this situation, after the server, our waiter and the manager all apologized; one would think that the very next burger off the grill would have come to us. Instead, they continued to serve the entire table next to us (the one that got our initial burger and the one that ordered 30 minutes after we did).  So we waited and waited and ordered more wine. My wine was wrong (I didn't complain) and my friend got a wine she didn't order (they gave it to her at no charge anyway so we didn't complain).
After waiting an insane amount of time we got our burger. It was good. We got pimento cheese, onions and bacon on it. The meat had a great taste as did each topping, individually. The big downfall was that it was quite greasy and the bun had a lot of grease soaked into it.  The truffle fries were...ok. Tasted like they had sat under a warmer for far too long.
The waiter came and asked if we wanted the check shortly after we finished. I thought it was odd because things had been so slow...now it felt like he was trying to push us along. But we were ready, so we said sure. We watched him walk around for 15 minutes talking to people and doing who knows what while we waited for our check. We finally had to go up to the hostess and ask to pay. We did express our concerns about the slowness then. At that point we had spent 2 hours in the restaurant since the time we sat down at our table. That's a lot of time for one hamburger and two appetizers.
In the end they did give us two desserts for free that I had selected to purchase for home. The carrot cake was wonderful. Moist and delicious.  The pumpkin cake was the opposite. Dry and dull.
I will go back at some point to give them another try, but next time I'll go ahead and prepare to have a long evening just in case.
One of our long time favorite groceries! Perfect for gift baskets and dinners!!
Great place with great food choices!  I love the atmosphere, the food is delicious!   The staff is friendly and provide great service.  They have specials all throughout the week.
Great food, atmosphere and outdoor dining. Ellie Morgan plays on Friday nights and she added a beautiful touch to our dining experience.
Dee is amazing she made my best friends birthday so special. She went over and above. Thank you Dee!🎉🙌❤️🎂

The management and staff was ignoring people coming in without face masks. The order that that I placed, my husband copied, and both burgers came out different and burned. They were both missing toppings. The staff seemed to be bothered when asking for things. After dinner we walked the store to purchase dessert and I was told I wasn’t able to get a fresh cookie that was sitting on a cart. Then another employee said I could. I went to purchase chicken salad and they turned off the lights before I was asked if I needed anything. And to top it off I went to cash out and the supervisor  said I couldn’t use cash because of the time of night. It was 7:15 pm on a Friday night. We have been here numerous times and we are very disappointed.
Amazing dinner like always.  We love how the staff really goes above and beyond. Great live music as well.
Wonderful boutique gourmet shopping at it's finest

During the pandemic, I have given this place numerous opportunities to prove they are taking safety seriously and almost each time they have failed! The first time I witnessed this was near the beginning of the pandemic, the end of March. We ordered to-go food and went inside to pick it up. Spoke with the supervisor at the register to get the order and he had on a pair of gloves that he used to check people out, touch the computer screen, accept money, open closet doors, check to go orders (not touch the food, but open the boxes) and he never changed the gloves between any of these tasks. Also, during this visit, there was an employee in the kitchen with no gloves on, touching different cooking utensils, and talking on his cell phone, all at the same time. I emailed the store about what I witnessed and the owner of Reid's emailed me back to apologize and say he was going to address the issues.

After this assurance that things would be corrected from the owner, we went back a few times and things got better. Then on 6/7 I went in to purchase some meat and the guy who was going to help me, first reached under his apron to pull out his phone and do something on it, put it back in his pocket, not wash his hands or put on gloves, then proceed to pick up the utensils to get the meat out of the cooler. I told him no thank you and just left. I let the owner know of this issue as well.

Now on 8/11, we decide to try the dine-in option in hopes they have figured out how to safely operate. To start the visit, there was a very unfriendly person working at the host stand, apparently this was the manager. This started the visit off on the wrong foot. Then after being sat, it took forever for the server to come greet us. We place our order and while waiting, I notice the bartender is standing on the edge of the bar, with no face covering on at all, speaking with a customer that is less than 6 feet away from him. This, in combination with having a completely inattentive server and not getting our order correct, just made for a very bad visit.

Later, I see him wear a shield that is connected to glass, but still no mask on. This essentially provides no protection to him or the guests he is serving as droplets and virus can spread under the shield. For the third time, I let the owner know of this ongoing issue, and he replies a very short and non-apologetic response. This makes me feel that him, nor any of the staff, understand the severity of these issues and they are not going to make changes to stop this from happening.

Lastly, I think between our 6/7 and 8/11 visits, they had an employee test positive for COVID-19 (per an email sent out to customers) and shut down to have the other employees tested and the place sanitized. You would think that this would make the leadership team take things far more seriously, but sadly they haven’t.

Food was good, as was the service, but my wife and I wound up with with each other's burgers due to a kitchen error.  (My toppings wound up on the medium rare burger which my wife ordered, and her toppings wound up on the medium well burger that I ordered.)  And the place is loud and cordial.

But we'll be back for half-price burgers on some future Tuesday.  Recommended!
Great food when they get it right.  The host or server who answers the phone cannot get the order correct.  Have ordered 3 times and 3 times the order have been wrong. Not sure I will patron again.
Great food, good service and nice atmosphere.

The food at Reid's is amazing for starters. Fresh and deliciously prepared. Very cool restaurant and store/market vibe as well. I'll be honest, not everything was perfect on our last to go order as they were out of an item and the salad we got was a bit small. However, 5 stars because they care about their customers and made it right. We will go here as often as we can and know our mouths are always in for a treat. That steak burger and brussels are from heaven.
Excellent as usual, loved the new coffee service
One of the best places to eat lunch in Charlotte.

I ordered 2 sandwiches; ( build your own sandwich $9.99 ea) one on ciabatta bread which was very dry. I also purchased the classic pound cake ($21.99) which had no taste and needed more vanilla flavoring and sugar.
Best darn burger I've had in a while! Wonderful service as well. The desserts are all gorgeous and prices are reasonable for all of it! My new fav
The customer service is always awesome.  Great selection, one of the best delis in Charlotte; the food is wonderful!

Our family used to come here for dinner regularly, at least once a month. Sunday brunch was a fave, too. Our last dinner here was in the early fall 2019 and the food was not as good and well-cooked as we were used to for Reid's. I remember specifically, among other things, their Beer-cheese pretzel bites were bland and clearly not up to their usual standards. It was a busy night (though that shouldn't be an excuse), so I figured it was an off day for them. Well, today we decided to have brunch here. It's not cheap, $25/adult, half price for kids. We were seated at noon and nearly half of the hot breakfast food was gone and not being replenished. It took 30 minutes to get pancakes and eggs Benedict. Clean plates were running out. The pastry offerings were not as varied. It just felt so chaotic. They've always been this busy during brunch, but everything used to run very smoothly and we didn't have to wait this long for the food stations to be replenished. I hate to see this change and I'm sadly hesitant to go back anytime soon....

reviews_data.owner_answer: Thank you for your feedback. I would greatly appreciate a call to discuss. We strive to provide an exceptional customer experience and apparently dropped the ball last night. Please email me at tom@reids and I will send you my cell #. Thank You
reviews_data.review_text:
I am so disappointed at the dining experience my family and I had tonight celebrating my birthday at Reid's.   Because I frequent Reids at the Selwyn Road location, I am familiar with their prepared foods and such, which led me to believe that dining there would be equal if not better than taking out.    I could not be more wrong.  The menu is well balanced with lots of great options, indicative of Reid's; substantial fare, but on occasion, with a slight twist.  One thing I felt confident in before tonight is the food would be well-prepared.

When we arrived, the person behind the meat counter was sneezing and did not follow proper health practices by covering his nose and mouth, nor did he wash his hands immediately after.   That is a major health code violation, but not enough to make me leave.  I know....  For simplification, I am  going to write out in bullet form the pros and cons of what followed  and let you, the reader decide if these things matter to you:
1.  I ordered my steak rare, and when it came out beyond medium, and I brought it to the server's attention, he mentioned then that, "this happens a lot.  I hit the rare button, but even when I do, they overcook it."  That was it, no apology, just an acknowledgment of, "yep, that is what happens!"
2.  We ordered two salmon entrees, which was highly recommended by the server as one of his favorites.  They were to be prepared medium and arrived well over medium and not fresh.  The portion was also relatively small.
3.  We ordered dessert, and our plates were removed before my son-in-law decided to finish mine, so I ask our server for another fork.  He proceeded to dig through the dirty dishes, trying to find his fork.  At that point, I was irritated and told him to please get him a clean fork.
4.  My daughter ordered an after-dinner cocktail, which she left 1/2 unfinished, because either she was over the entire experience, and it was time to go, or because it was not a good drink.  I tasted it and agreed it was lame with a little bourbon and resembled a house drink.

In conclusion,  I believe there is one of two things that happened.  We dined here for dinner on an off-night, and if so, okay, things happen.  Sadly though, we will not go back.  We will not throw away another $200+ to see if it is better or even slightly better.  The other possibility is this is the place to go for lunch.  Maybe these great reviews are from lunchtime diners??  I guess that if the dinner experience is consistent with mine, there are far too many good restaurants around this area that will eventually leave Reid's to rely on prepared foods, lunch dining, and Postmates.  By the way, one last miss, the manager never came over to apologize for the steak miss, which leads me to believe they do not care about the dining-in aspect of their business model any longer.

UPDATE January 2020.  I am updating my review to note that the owner and I had a nice conversation following my review.  He was diligent in connecting, but I am glad we did.  He listened to my feedback and was genuinely concerned about my feelings and regretted this happened on my birthday.  The comment he made that meant the most was how much reviews impacted his team.  This said a lot to me about how much he cares about his associate's feelings, and that he must instill a sense of ownership in them.

He reimbursed me for my dinner, which I appreciate very much.  I have not been back to dine, but maybe I will on my birthday!  :-)  Needless to say, this is an owner that cares about his establishment's reputation and more importantly, his team.  I wish him much success.
Awesome Salmon BLT and a super friendly staff

Had a terrible meal today at Reid’s. My wife and I each ordered a burger medium and both came burnt/well done. Mine came with 2 small pieces of onion so I asked for more. They brought 2 more small pieces...guess they can’t afford a full slice on a $15 burger. Very disappointed and won’t be back.
Bakery meets deli for all your needs!

Great specials all week and half-priced bottles of wine on Saturday. Burgers on Tuesdays! Glasses of wine on Wednesdays!

Their burgers are excellent, the charcuterie is awesome and a great deal, salads excellent, truffle fries! I prefer to make my own salad at the bar and you can also make your sandwich to order. A great place to have lunch!

Music on Weds nights. Great outdoor seating.

Awesome gluten free treats. Great hostess gifts. And the quiche in the freezer is AMAZING! As are the frozen biscuits. You're welcome!

Great wine selection.

Also-- great bartenders and servers.

I have been to this location before.  It is always very clean and the staff here are welcoming and friendly.  It is better suited as a specialty grocery store where you can pick up something for a special lunch or dinner to prepare at home. However they do have a good but very small selection of premade salads and sandwiches. They are fresh and delicious and I will return here again.

Fantastic burgers and friendly atmosphere. Being a restaurant, there are often many new faces coming in and out, but the familiar faces are Reid’s greatest strength. Pete, the bartender, is hands-down one of the best around! We don’t even make it to the seating area before Pete spots us and puts in our order. He has a fantastic memory and truly cares about his guests. We loved the two bartenders who worked at Reid’s before Pete (Paisley and Christine) and when they left we thought no one could compare. Luckily, Pete is the best and that’s all there is to it! Plus, he always remembers to bring me a napkin to stash a little bacon for my pup. :) The “fine foods” selection is full of delicious local goodies that double as great gifts. Thank you, Reid’s!

Stopped in and got a few different marinated chicken breasts to grill at home. Hands down, best chicken I ever had! They put it through a machine that makes it nice and thin. Full of flavor! Will only get chicken here for now on!

New menu is really disappointing. Removed all the great items such as the brussel sprouts with chorizo and the blackened salmon.

Was our favorite place, hopefully they can go back to the the fabulous menu they changed.

We used Reids catering for our work luncheon. Hannah, who was my point of contact, was  extremely delightful, patient and professional during the entire planning process. She worked within our budget and provided a catered event of delicious meats.
I thanked Hanna for returning my calls and text messages in a such a timely manner. I would definitely recommend them for catered events!

For 7 days, Reid's Fine Foods furnished boxed lunches for the 5-6 volunteers staffing the Will Call office of the Wells Fargo Championship and they were great!   Every day, I heard compliments on the food and, as the supervisor, I found the convenience & friendliness to be world-class.   Thank you to Monique, Dannie, Ericka and the entire Reid's staff for great lunches every day of the tournament!
05/05/2019 - I did the same thing for the 2019 Wells Fargo Championship and, once again, Reid's provided stellar, first-class service.   Their boxed lunches are wonderful and the customer service could not be better!   Thank you to the entire Reid's staff!
A Charlotte institution and tradition.
Wonderful place and delicious food
Nice place, nice burguer. It is a market and restaurant. You can grab a wine in the market and drink with your food in the restaurant. Amazing experience.

Nice outdoor atmosphere and good food. The tuna poke lettuce wraps we're good but not sure if it was worth $14. The crab cakes were ok, not jumbo lump meat but a good amount of crab. The gluten free pistachio cheese cake was tasty with a crumb-like crust. They offer half off liquors and cocktails on Thursdays so I got an old fashioned with dinner.
